result.content: FINAL_ANSWER: To reconstruct the sequence of sibling-merge operations, perform a post-order traversal of the final tree. For each node u in the final tree, identify its children v1, v2, ..., vk. For each child vi, determine if it was formed by a merge operation. If a child vi represents a merge of original children c1, c2, ..., cm from the initial tree, record the sequence of binary merges used to create vi: (c1, c2), ((c1, c2), c3), ..., ((...(c1, c2), ...), cm). By processing nodes in post-order, we ensure that all merge operations required to form the subtrees are recorded before the operations required to form the current node u are recorded. This guarantees a valid topological ordering of operations. JUSTIFICATION: A post-order traversal ensures that all descendant nodes (and their corresponding merge operations) are processed and added to the sequence before their parent nodes, satisfying the dependency requirements of the tree structure.
